title: The Quintessential Quintuplets
overview: Uesugi Fuutarou lands a tutoring job that promises five times the normal pay. As it turns out, it’s going to take more than five times the work.

title: Rooftop Confession
overview: Futaro proposes an exam to settle the debate of whether or not the quintuplets need a tutor.

title: A Mountain of Problems
overview: Nino isn't happy that Futaro is beginning to get along with her sisters, so she takes matters into her own hands.

title: A Day Off
overview: Futaro receives his first pay, and decides to take Raiha to the arcade, where she’s always wanted to go.

title: Five Fifths
overview: The fireworks have started, but the girls are still split up. Ichika wanders off on her own, and Futaro goes after her.

title: What's Been Built Up
overview: The midterm exams are coming up, and the girls' father set Futaro up to a seemingly impossible task.

title: Liar McLieface
overview: Futaro spent the night at the quintuplets' apartment. He wakes up to an unexpected situation.

title: The Photo That Started It All
overview: Futaro falls ill after Miku feeds him a hefty serving of croquettes. Nino makes the situation even worse than it needs to be.

title: Legend of Fate Day 1
overview: School is abuzz about the camp school trip coming up. Meanwhile, Raiha catches the flu.

title: Legend of Fate Day 2
overview: Futaro and the girls arrive at camp school. His outfit for the test of courage causes more than just a scare for Nino.

title: Legend of Fate Day 3
overview: Ichika and Futaro get locked in the storage shed. The others are concerned about their absence, but not necessarily for the same reasons.

title: Legend of Fate Day 2000
overview: It’s the last day of camp school. Itsuki is missing. Futaro thinks he knows where to find her, but he’s sick and shouldn’t be wandering in the snow.

title: Quite the Crappy Kyoto Quagmire
overview: Futaro is hospitalized for a while after falling sick at camp school. He returns to work tutoring the quintuplets, only to realize someone has fallen way behind.

title: Seven Goodbyes (1)
overview: There’s one week left to the term final. Futaro and the sisters are set on passing this time, but Nino and Miku can’t seem to get along.

title: Seven Goodbyes (2)
overview: Nino lets a dejected Futaro into her hotel room. Futaro tells her about how he’d run into the girl he met in Kyoto five years prior.

title: Seven Goodbyes (3)
overview: After discovering Yotsuba wants to quit the Track team but can't tell her teammates, Uesugi disguises Itsuki to resign for her.

title: Good Work Today
overview: Uesugi runs into the Quintuplets while out shopping. They invite him to visit their new home with an ulterior motive.

title: The Last Exam
overview: Miku is desperately trying to learn how to make chocolates for Uesugi. Meanwhile, preparations for the Final exams are intensifying, but Uesugi decides its time for a study break.

title: Begin the Offensive
overview: The quintuplet's father attempts to convince Nino to come back with him, but Uesugi rides in to save the day and Nino makes a confession. The quintuplets then celebrate their exam achievements with desserts. Later, everyone enjoys a vacation at a hot springs resort.

title: Scrambled Eggs
overview: Uesugi enlists the "real" Itsuki to help him discover who was impersonating her yesterday. After discovering a room full of Itsuki, he interrogates them one by one. Uesugi struggles to tell which quintuplet is which.

title: Welcome to Class 3-1
overview: The quintuplets each find a job to help Ichika pay for the rent of their new place. They each settle into their new jobs and their new classmates.

title: Five Cranes in Return
overview: The quintuplets’ father tries to get Futaro’s classmate, Takeda, to take over as their tutor. They make a bet.

title: Sisters War (1)
overview: Ichika, Nino and Itsuki plot ways to make a move on Futaro during the school trip. Yotsuba is troubled by their change in attitude

title: Sisters War (2)
overview: With desperation increasing, things become tense amongst the sisters as well as with Futaro. For their elective tour on the last day of the school trip, the quintuplets decide to keep things fair by leaving the outcome to luck.
